exact_route_path: grouped_rows -> filtered_groups -> ordered_groups -> page_groups -> paged_rows JSON envelope

table_reads_or_sources: public.buyer_summary_inbox_search_v1 only when p_search is not null/stale projection check
joins: join v_proposals_summary for latest proposal lifecycle of rejected candidates
left join reject_context_latest for rejected item context
left join buyer_summary_inbox_search_v1 for search projection when p_search is present
join page_groups on request_id after global grouping/order
filters: optional search text, null for page25 load route
optional p_company_id, null for load route
query_shape.pagination: group offset pagination using row_number() group_index; page25 is offset 0 limit 25
order_by: ordered_groups: request_id_old desc nulls last, latest_created_at desc nulls last, request_id desc
paged row_rank: created_at desc nulls last, request_item_id asc
expected_indexes: buyer_summary_inbox_search_v1 request_id index and trigram search index for filtered path
missing/proposed: proposal_items request_item_id::text + coalesce(updated_at, created_at) desc + id desc include reject context columns

root_cause_classification.offset_pagination: not_primary_for_page25_offset0; deeper pages remain a risk
root_cause_classification.n_plus_one: not_in_route_path; single RPC invocation per load target sample
root_cause_classification.wide_select: not_primary; payload around 14KB and explicit JSON fields
root_cause_classification.cold_cache: not_primary; repeated wave29/wave30 250 samples stay high with errors=0

conclusion: The page25 bottleneck is server-side query-shape CPU/plan pressure: first-page route still builds and counts the full gated/grouped inbox, then enriches rejected rows through proposal joins before returning 25 rows.
